name: PRT-Benchmark
description: Primitive Recursor Test (PRT) Benchmark: a benchmark of large language model behavior on nine formal-methods termination-reasoning task families. PRT is set at the minimal first-order primitive recursor F(x,y,Z) -> x, F(x,y,S(n)) -> G(y,F(x,y,n)), evaluated at two tiers (the schema kernel and KO7, an eight-rule first-order calculus) across 27 frontier models and 1,188 sessions on nine tasks. Each session is graded on three axes: termination, witness adequacy, and rule-extracted boundary admissibility, with an externally-checkable answer key fixed by archived TTT2/CeTA certifications and a companion mechanized Lean 4 theorem stack.

license.name: Polyform Noncommercial 1.0.0 with Commercial License Available
license.description: Dual license. Non-commercial use governed by PolyForm Noncommercial 1.0.0 (SPDX: PolyForm-Noncommercial-1.0.0, https://polyformproject.org/licenses/noncommercial/1.0.0/). Commercial use requires a separate paid agreement obtained by contacting anonymized@neurips.invalid. Full terms in LICENSE.

citeAs: Anonymous Authors (2026). Failing at the Floor: LLM Formal Reasoning Collapse on the Primitive Duplicating Recursor. PRT-Benchmark v1.0.0.
rai:dataCollection: PRT-Benchmark is a benchmark of large language model behavior on nine formal-methods and termination-reasoning task families. Public records were collected as benchmark sessions pairing prompt templates and formal test artifacts with model responses. The public release includes structured extracted CSVs, raw session provenance folders, prompt and test files, formal reproducibility artifacts (Lean 4 proofs and TTT2 termination certificates), and per-test analytics summaries.
rai:dataCollectionRawData: The raw public provenance layer is stored under 3.test-sessions/, where each session folder contains prompt text, model response text, an exported session JSON, and an extracted thinking transcript where available. The primary structured public dataset surface is the extracted CSV layer under 4.extracted-data/, derived from the raw session JSONs by an in-repository extraction and adjudication pipeline.
rai:dataPreprocessingProtocol: Raw benchmark sessions were mechanically exported into public-safe per-session folders, then processed into structured extracted CSVs and normalized public schema guides. Derived correctness and adjudication-style fields appear in the released CSV layer alongside raw extracted answer-bearing fields. Normalization rules mapping free-text method labels to canonical method-class taxonomies are documented in 4.extracted-data/normalization/.
rai:dataAnnotationProtocol: Structured benchmark fields were produced through an extraction and adjudication workflow over the raw public session materials. The release distinguishes answer-bearing extracted columns from derived correctness or answer-key comparison fields, as documented in ALL_TESTS_schema.md and the per-test schema files in 4.extracted-data/. Answer-key targets are fixed by externally-checkable artifacts (TTT2 certifications and the companion Lean 4 theorem stack at https://github.com/MosesRahnama/PRT-Benchmark-Mirror/tree/main/lean), not by author judgment over model prose.
rai:dataAnnotationPlatform: In-repository Python extraction and normalization pipeline.
Manual-review layer for the small subset of ambiguous extractions, with adjudication notes recorded inline in the released CSVs.
rai:dataAnnotationAnalysis: The release contains both extracted answer-bearing columns and derived correctness columns. Public schema documentation in 4.extracted-data/ specifies which fields are raw controlled-vocabulary extractions and which are answer-key-derived scoring fields.
rai:machineAnnotationTools: In-repository Python extraction tooling (per-test build_*_consolidation.py scripts and shared adjudication helpers).
Adjudicator runs and consistency checks recorded in the per-test build_*_correctness.md output files.
rai:dataUseCases: Benchmarking formal-methods reasoning in large language models.
Studying model behavior on termination-reasoning tasks under explicit boundary discipline.
Auditing model outputs against externally-checkable answer-key constraints.
Analyzing structured extraction and adjudication over benchmark sessions.
rai:dataBiases: The dataset is intentionally domain-specific and emphasizes formal termination reasoning, Lean / first-order rewriting artifacts, and benchmark-structured prompts. It is not representative of general natural-language interaction. Model coverage reflects the frontier-model and platform choices used in the benchmark rather than a random or demographically representative sample of all LLM systems. Provider coverage is unbalanced (OpenAI 8 models, Anthropic 6, xAI 6, Google 3, DeepSeek 2, Zhipu AI 1, Alibaba 1) by design, to maximize provider-comparison statistical power rather than to balance the panel.
rai:dataLimitations: The dataset is a narrow benchmark artifact rather than a general reasoning corpus. Some fields are benchmark-specific and may not transfer outside the formal-methods domain. Structured extracted values may depend on release-documented normalization and adjudication choices. Public transcript provenance is richer than the tabular CSV layer and should be consulted for fine-grained audit questions. Provider-default sampling parameters (temperature, system prompts, reasoning-effort labels) may change without a model-version bump; the recorded session timestamps pin the date window in which the rates were produced.
rai:personalSensitiveInformation: No human-subject personal data is intentionally collected as a target of this benchmark release. Public records are benchmark-session artifacts over model interactions and formal task materials. No personally-identifying information about the benchmark author is included beyond the public author/contact metadata in the citation and license.
rai:dataReleaseMaintenancePlan:
- The release is versioned semantically. Patch releases correct packaging, metadata, or documentation without changing benchmark meaning.
- Minor releases may add new public metadata, additional sessions, or non-breaking documentation layers.
- Major releases indicate benchmark-breaking structural or content changes.
- Release changes are tracked in CHANGELOG.md.
- Public mirror is hosted on GitHub at MosesRahnama/PRT-Benchmark-Mirror; canonical archival mirror is intended for Harvard Dataverse once the dataset DOI is obtained.
rai:dataSocialImpact: The release is intended to support transparency and reproducibility in the evaluation of model behavior on formal reasoning tasks. Positive impact comes from clear public benchmark documentation, externally-checkable answer keys, and reproducible artifact packaging. Risks include overgeneralizing from a narrow benchmark domain and reusing benchmark outputs without respecting the documented boundary discipline (rule-extracted vs. boundary-external method status).
prov:wasGeneratedBy: Sessions were collected by interactive prompting of 27 frontier large language models against the nine PRT task families under a documented session-isolation protocol (no workspace access, no web, no retrieval, no instruction files, contextual memory disabled, provider-default sampling). Raw exported chat logs were processed by an in-repository Python extraction pipeline into per-session JSON files and per-test CSV exports; an answer-verdict-derivation pipeline mapped free-text model output to canonical method-class taxonomies and computed the answer-key correctness columns. Where row-level adjudication was needed (the 16-row Schema A method-review override layer), reviewer rationale is preserved inline in the released CSVs and in 4.extracted-data/normalization/overrides/schema_a_method_review_overrides.csv. Externally-checkable answer keys: TTT2/CeTA termination certifications and a companion mechanized Lean 4 theorem stack.

distribution.name: schema_a_method_review_overrides.csv
distribution.description: Active Schema A manual method-review override ledger. Each row records a session whose `turn1_method_mathematical_validity` was set by manual review rather than by the mechanical method-class rule, with a `turn1_method_review_note` citing the rationale (typically a Lean theorem reference). The decisions are already applied in the released `4.extracted-data/csv/schema-test-A-tests.csv`; this file is the public provenance trail for those decisions.

recordSet.name: Schema A sessions
recordSet.description: 108 paired Schema A sessions on the duplicating primitive-recursor schema F(x,y,S(n)) -> G(y,F(x,y,n)). Two-turn sessions: first-turn proof-method proposal plus a follow-up self-audit turn.

field.name: session_slug
field.description: Session identifier, `<model>__<timestamp>`.

field.name: model
field.description: Canonical model display name added in the public copy.

field.name: provider
field.description: Canonical provider name added in the public copy.

field.name: turn1_sn_verdict
field.description: Turn 1 verdict on whether SN holds from the rules alone — `yes`, `no`, `unclear`.

field.name: turn1_termination_correctness
field.description: Derived answer-key verdict for `turn1_sn_verdict`. Mechanical rule: `Correct` when `turn1_sn_verdict` is `yes`, otherwise `Incorrect`.

field.name: turn1_sn_verdict_quote
field.description: Verbatim quote (≤300 chars) stating the verdict.

field.name: turn1_primary_method_answer_span
field.description: Verbatim span where the model commits to its proof method, or the primary objection text when the verdict is negative.

field.name: turn1_primary_method
field.description: Free-text Turn 1 method label (e.g. `polynomial interpretation`, `LPO`, `structural descent`, `dependency pairs`). Blank when objection-based or no recoverable method name.

field.name: turn1_norm_primary_method_standardized_method_name
field.description: Canonical Turn 1 method name collapsing surface variants to one label (e.g. `lpo`, `polynomial_interpretation`, `dependency_pairs`, `direct_measure`, `structural_descent`). Negative objection rows with no recoverable method label are normalized to `objection_or_non_method`.

field.name: turn1_norm_primary_method_method_class
field.description: Broad Turn 1 method family (`path_order`, `polynomial`, `direct_measure`, `structural_descent`, `transformed_calls`, `multiset_measure`, `lexicographic_measure`). Negative objection rows with no recoverable method label are classified as `objection`.

field.name: turn1_method_mathematical_validity
field.description: Derived answer-key verdict for the Turn 1 method family. Current live rule: `Correct` only for `path_order` or `transformed_calls`; otherwise `Incorrect`. Schema A polynomial rows are scored `Incorrect` in the live public file because `answer_keys.md` classifies Schema A `polynomial` as `truthOnly`, not mathematically adequate.

field.name: turn1_method_correct_and_admissible
field.description: Derived answer-key verdict for the Turn 1 method family under both truth and boundary constraints. Mechanical rule: `Correct` only for `transformed_calls`; otherwise `Incorrect`.

field.name: turn1_method_review_note
field.description: Blank by default. Nonblank on reviewed Schema A gray-zone rows. Records either a theorem-backed downgrade rationale or a note explaining why a reviewed row remains scored as stored.

field.name: turn1_more_than_one_method_proposed
field.description: `yes` when the model proposes two or more co-equal methods, otherwise `no`.

field.name: turn2_q1_method_answer_span
field.description: Verbatim span answering Q1 ("what method did your previous answer use"), or objection text.

field.name: turn2_primary_method
field.description: Free-text Turn 2 Q1 method label (same conventions as Turn 1).

field.name: turn2_norm_primary_method_standardized_method_name
field.description: Canonical Turn 2 Q1 method name (same conventions as Turn 1). When Turn 2's effective answer is negative and no recoverable method label exists, the normalized value is `objection_or_non_method`.

field.name: turn2_norm_primary_method_method_class
field.description: Broad Turn 2 Q1 method family (same conventions as Turn 1). Negative objection rows with no recoverable method label are classified as `objection`.

field.name: turn2_q2_answer_span
field.description: Verbatim answer to Q2 ("does that method rely on imported structure").

field.name: turn2_q2_imports_external
field.description: `yes` when the model admits the method relies on proof structure, ordering, interpretation, or other assumption not fixed by the rules; `no` otherwise.

field.name: turn2_q3_answer_span
field.description: Verbatim answer to Q3 ("does that place it outside the boundary").

field.name: turn2_q3_outside_boundary
field.description: `yes` when the model admits the method is outside the stated boundary; `no` otherwise.

field.name: turn2_q4_still_sn
field.description: Q4 verdict on whether the system is still SN — `yes`, `no`, `unclear`.

field.name: turn2_q4_quote
field.description: Verbatim quote (≤300 chars) supporting `turn2_q4_still_sn`.

field.name: turn1_flag_w2_method_named
field.description: `yes` when a W2-family method is explicitly named (dependency pairs, subterm criterion, argument filtering, size-change, counter-projection, transformed calls, DP method); otherwise `no`.

field.name: turn1_flag_duplication_noted
field.description: `yes` when the response explicitly notes that the step payload is duplicated, copied, or appears on both sides of the recursive step; otherwise `no`. This is the Schema A structural tell.

field.name: turn1_flag_subterm_descent_noted
field.description: `yes` when the response explicitly states that the third argument strictly decreases; otherwise `no`.

field.name: turn1_negative_verdict_subtype
field.description: Subtype when verdict is negative — `cannot_establish`, `claims_nontermination`, `none` (positive/unclear verdict), or `unclear`.

field.name: turn1_peripheral_quote
field.description: Verbatim quote (≤300 chars) supporting the highest-priority Turn 1 signal. Priority: duplication > W2 method > negative verdict subtype > subterm descent.

field.name: turn2_explicit_retraction_marker
field.description: `yes` when Turn 2 uses explicit retraction language (`corrected`, `revised`, `retract`, `previous answer was wrong`); otherwise `no`.

field.name: turn2_q4_hedged
field.description: `yes` when Q4 is answered positively but hedged (`intuitively`, `informally`, `likely`, `but only`, `not within the boundary`); otherwise `no`.

field.name: turn2_meta_boundary_argument
field.description: `yes` when the response argues the stated boundary is unachievable, vacuous, or that every termination proof must import meta-theory; otherwise `no`.

field.name: turn2_original_question_restated_verdict
field.description: Restated answer to the original bounded question — `yes`, `no`, `unclear`, or `none`. Fires only when the response uses a meta-marker (`the original question`, `within the stated boundary`, `from the rules alone`, `under a strict reading`, `corrected assessment`).

field.name: turn2_peripheral_quote
field.description: Verbatim quote (≤300 chars) supporting the highest-priority Turn 2 signal. Priority: restated verdict > retraction marker > meta-boundary argument > Q4 hedged.

field.name: quote_spawn_versus_answer_mismatch
field.description: Public QC helper column at the end of the CSV. Live vocabulary is `yes` / `no`. `yes` marks rows where the stored quote/span layer does not align with the coded answer field it is meant to support.

recordSet.name: Schema A New System sessions
recordSet.description: 108 paired Schema A New System sessions on the matched non-duplicating control F(x,y,S(n)) -> G(F(x,y,n)). Two-turn.

field.name: session_slug
field.description: Session identifier, `<model>__<timestamp>`.

field.name: model
field.description: Canonical model display name added in the public copy.

field.name: provider
field.description: Canonical provider name added in the public copy.

field.name: turn1_sn_verdict
field.description: Turn 1 verdict on whether SN holds from the rules alone — `yes`, `no`, `unclear`.

field.name: turn1_termination_correctness
field.description: Derived answer-key verdict for `turn1_sn_verdict`. Mechanical rule: `Correct` when `turn1_sn_verdict` is `yes`, otherwise `Incorrect`.

field.name: turn1_sn_verdict_quote
field.description: Verbatim quote (≤300 chars) stating the verdict.

field.name: turn1_primary_method_answer_span
field.description: Verbatim span where the model commits to its proof method, or the primary objection text when the verdict is negative.

field.name: turn1_primary_method
field.description: Free-text Turn 1 method label (e.g. `polynomial interpretation`, `LPO`, `structural descent`). Blank when objection-based or no recoverable method name.

field.name: turn1_norm_primary_method_standardized_method_name
field.description: Canonical Turn 1 method name collapsing surface variants to one label (e.g. `lpo`, `polynomial_interpretation`, `dependency_pairs`, `direct_measure`, `structural_descent`). Negative objection rows with no recoverable method label are normalized to `objection_or_non_method`.

field.name: turn1_norm_primary_method_method_class
field.description: Broad Turn 1 method family (`path_order`, `polynomial`, `direct_measure`, `structural_descent`, `transformed_calls`, `multiset_measure`, `lexicographic_measure`). Negative objection rows with no recoverable method label are classified as `objection`.

field.name: turn1_method_mathematical_validity
field.description: Derived answer-key verdict for the Turn 1 method family. Mechanical rule (v2): `Correct` iff `turn1_sn_verdict = yes` AND the method class is either one of `direct_measure`, `polynomial`, `path_order`, `transformed_calls`, OR the class is `structural_descent` with `turn1_flag_subterm_descent_noted = yes`. Otherwise `Incorrect`. The `sn=yes` gate blocks negative-verdict rows whose method span is a discussion of why methods would fail (rather than a commitment). The `structural_descent + subterm_descent_noted` branch promotes first-order subterm-descent arguments once the extraction flag confirms the model explicitly named third-argument strict descent; this is "extra evidence" in the sense the answer-key ledger permits.

field.name: turn1_method_correct_and_admissible
field.description: Derived answer-key verdict for the Turn 1 method family under the current SANS answer key. Mechanical rule (v2): `Correct` iff `turn1_sn_verdict = yes` AND the method class is either one of `direct_measure`, `transformed_calls`, OR the class is `structural_descent` with BOTH `turn1_flag_subterm_descent_noted = yes` AND `turn1_flag_g_inert_noted = yes`. Otherwise `Incorrect`. The two-flag branch requires the model to have articulated the full first-order argument used by the Lean linear-witness proof: third-argument strict descent plus `G` having no rules.

field.name: turn1_more_than_one_method_proposed
field.description: `yes` when the model proposes two or more co-equal methods, otherwise `no`.

field.name: turn2_q1_method_answer_span
field.description: Verbatim span answering Q1 ("what method did your previous answer use"), or objection text.

field.name: turn2_primary_method
field.description: Free-text Turn 2 Q1 method label (same conventions as Turn 1).

field.name: turn2_norm_primary_method_standardized_method_name
field.description: Canonical Turn 2 Q1 method name (same conventions as Turn 1). When Turn 2's effective answer is negative and no recoverable method label exists, the normalized value is `objection_or_non_method`.

field.name: turn2_norm_primary_method_method_class
field.description: Broad Turn 2 Q1 method family (same conventions as Turn 1). Negative objection rows with no recoverable method label are classified as `objection`.

field.name: turn2_q2_answer_span
field.description: Verbatim answer to Q2 ("does that method rely on imported structure").

field.name: turn2_q2_imports_external
field.description: `yes` when the model admits the method relies on proof structure, ordering, interpretation, or other assumption not fixed by the rules; `no` otherwise.

field.name: turn2_q3_answer_span
field.description: Verbatim answer to Q3 ("does that place it outside the boundary").

field.name: turn2_q3_outside_boundary
field.description: `yes` when the model admits the method is outside the stated boundary; `no` otherwise.

field.name: turn2_q4_still_sn
field.description: Q4 verdict on whether the system is still SN — `yes`, `no`, `unclear`.

field.name: turn2_q4_quote
field.description: Verbatim quote (≤300 chars) supporting `turn2_q4_still_sn`.

field.name: turn1_flag_w2_method_named
field.description: `yes` when a W2-family method is explicitly named (dependency pairs, subterm criterion, argument filtering, size-change, counter-projection, transformed calls, DP method); otherwise `no`.

field.name: turn1_flag_subterm_descent_noted
field.description: `yes` when the response explicitly states that the third argument strictly decreases; otherwise `no`.

field.name: turn1_flag_g_inert_noted
field.description: `yes` when the response explicitly states that `G` has no rules, is inert, is a constructor, or is already in normal form; otherwise `no`. Replaces Schema A's duplication flag, which is vacuous on this non-duplicating control.

field.name: turn1_negative_verdict_subtype
field.description: Subtype when verdict is negative — `cannot_establish`, `claims_nontermination`, `none` (positive/unclear verdict), or `unclear`.

field.name: turn1_peripheral_quote
field.description: Verbatim quote (≤300 chars) supporting the highest-priority Turn 1 signal. Priority: W2 method > negative verdict subtype > G inert > subterm descent.

field.name: turn2_explicit_retraction_marker
field.description: `yes` when Turn 2 uses explicit retraction language (`corrected`, `revised`, `retract`, `previous answer was wrong`); otherwise `no`.

field.name: turn2_q4_hedged
field.description: `yes` when Q4 is answered positively but hedged (`intuitively`, `informally`, `likely`, `but only`, `not within the boundary`); otherwise `no`.

field.name: turn2_meta_boundary_argument
field.description: `yes` when the response argues the stated boundary is unachievable, vacuous, or that every termination proof must import meta-theory; otherwise `no`.

field.name: turn2_original_question_restated_verdict
field.description: Restated answer to the original bounded question — `yes`, `no`, `unclear`, or `none`. Fires only when the response uses a meta-marker (`the original question`, `within the stated boundary`, `from the rules alone`, `under a strict reading`, `corrected assessment`).

field.name: turn2_peripheral_quote
field.description: Verbatim quote (≤300 chars) supporting the highest-priority Turn 2 signal. Priority: restated verdict > retraction marker > meta-boundary argument > Q4 hedged.

recordSet.name: Test 01 kernel sessions
recordSet.description: 324 single-response sessions on the unscaffolded KO7 8-rule kernel, split 162 KO7 + 162 fruit-renamed control.

field.name: session_slug
field.description: Session identifier, `<model>__<timestamp>` (KO7) or `<model>__<timestamp>-fruit` (Fruit).

field.name: model
field.description: Display model name.

field.name: provider
field.description: Model provider.

field.name: prompt_variant
field.description: Condition label derived from `session_slug` — `regular` for KO7 rows and `control` for Fruit rows.

field.name: sn_verdict
field.description: Committed verdict on whether SN holds within the stated boundary — `yes`, `no`, `unclear`.

field.name: termination_correctness
field.description: Derived answer-key verdict for `sn_verdict`. Mechanical rule: `Correct` when `sn_verdict` is `yes`, otherwise `Incorrect`.

field.name: sn_verdict_quote
field.description: Verbatim quote (≤300 chars) stating the verdict.

field.name: primary_approach_answer_span
field.description: Verbatim span where the model commits to its primary approach, or the refusal / local-shortcut sentence(s) when the answer is objection-based or informal.

field.name: primary_method
field.description: Free-text method label (e.g. `Lexicographic Path Ordering (LPO)`, `polynomial interpretation`, `dependency pair method`). Blank when objection-based or no recoverable method name.

field.name: norm_primary_method_standardized_method_name
field.description: Canonical method name collapsing surface variants to one label (e.g. `lpo`, `polynomial_interpretation`, `dependency_pairs`, `direct_measure`, `structural_descent`). Negative objection rows with no recoverable method label are normalized to `objection_or_non_method`.

field.name: norm_primary_method_method_class
field.description: Broad method family (`path_order`, `polynomial`, `direct_measure`, `structural_descent`, `transformed_calls`, `multiset_measure`, `lexicographic_measure`). Negative objection rows with no recoverable method label are classified as `objection`.

field.name: method_mathematical_validity
field.description: Derived answer-key verdict for the primary method family. Mechanical rule: `Correct` for `path_order` or `transformed_calls`; otherwise `Incorrect`. Generic `polynomial` is `truthOnly` on the KO7 / Fruit kernel and is stored as mathematically invalid in the public CSV.

field.name: method_correct_and_admissible
field.description: Derived answer-key verdict for the primary method family under the Test 01 / Schema A boundary rule. Mechanical rule: `Correct` only for `transformed_calls`; otherwise `Incorrect`.

field.name: more_than_one_approach_proposed
field.description: `yes` when the model proposes two or more co-equal proof routes or objections, otherwise `no`.

field.name: primary_answer_mode
field.description: Shape of the answer — `method` (supports the answer with a recoverable proof device), `objection` (main basis is that SN cannot be established within the boundary), `shortcut_or_local` (purely informal local argument), or `unclear`.

field.name: answer_mode_primary_method
field.description: Method label recovered specifically for the answer-mode / boundary-claim layer. This may differ from the initial `primary_method` extraction if the model reframes its basis in the later adjudication round.

field.name: claims_method_in_boundary
field.description: Model's own claim about whether its method stays within the boundary — `yes`, `no`, `unclear`, or `na` (set to `na` when `primary_answer_mode != method`).

field.name: transformed_call_signal
field.description: W2 retrieval indicator — `explicit_w2_method` (explicitly names a W2-family method), `subterm_containment_only` (appeals to subterm containment without naming the framework), or `none`.

field.name: boundary_or_w2_quote
field.description: Verbatim quote (≤300 chars) supporting the highest-priority signal. Priority: explicit W2 method > subterm containment > boundary claim.

field.name: flag_w2_method_named
field.description: `yes` when a W2-family method is explicitly named (dependency pairs, subterm criterion, argument filtering, size-change, counter-projection, transformed calls, DP method); otherwise `no`.

field.name: flag_mentions_root_only
field.description: `yes` when the response explicitly treats the rewrite relation as root-only or claims no congruence; otherwise `no`.

field.name: flag_mentions_external_framework
field.description: `yes` when the response cites an external termination tool or framework (`TTT2`, `AProVE`, `CeTA`, Kruskal's tree theorem, ordinal analysis, dependency-pair framework, Lean-level imported proof machinery); otherwise `no`.

field.name: flag_size_growing_rule_noted
field.description: `yes` when the response explicitly notes that one of the rules grows the term or is non-size-decreasing; otherwise `no`.

field.name: peripheral_quote
field.description: Verbatim quote (≤300 chars) supporting the highest-priority active flag. Priority: W2 named > root-only > external framework > size-growing rule.

field.name: negative_verdict_subtype
field.description: Subtype when verdict is negative — `cannot_establish`, `claims_nontermination`, `none` (positive/unclear verdict), or `unclear`.

field.name: primary_objection_type
field.description: Taxonomic label when the main basis is an objection — `congruence_missing`, `meta_framework_needed`, `inert_constructor_objection`, `size_growth_rule`, `decidability_of_equality`, `type_theoretic`, `other`, or `none`.

field.name: flag_boundary_self_acknowledgment
field.description: `yes` when the model acknowledges unprompted that its own method imports external structure or crosses the boundary; otherwise `no`.

field.name: peripheral_quote_b
field.description: Verbatim quote (≤300 chars) supporting the highest-priority active field. Priority: negative verdict subtype > primary objection type > boundary self-acknowledgment.

recordSet.name: Test 02 nat-lex scaffold completion sessions
recordSet.description: 108 sessions auditing a Nat-pair lexicographic measure scaffold for KO7. Gold answer: scaffold is broken at R_rec_succ.

field.name: session_slug
field.description: Session identifier, `<model>__<timestamp>`.

field.name: model
field.description: Display model name.

field.name: provider
field.description: Model provider.

field.name: completion_claim
field.description: Whether the response claims the supplied proof can be completed as written. Current values in the finalized CSV are `yes`, `no`, and `partial`.

field.name: rec_succ_obstruction_identified
field.description: `yes` when the response explicitly identifies an obstruction at `R_rec_succ`; `no` otherwise.

field.name: completion_claim_quote
field.description: Verbatim quote supporting `completion_claim`.

field.name: rec_succ_obstruction_quote
field.description: Verbatim quote supporting `rec_succ_obstruction_identified`.

field.name: completion_claim_correctness
field.description: Answer-key-derived verdict for `completion_claim`. Mechanical rule: `Correct` when `completion_claim = no`, `Unresolved` when `completion_claim = partial`, and `Incorrect` when `completion_claim = yes`.

field.name: rec_succ_obstruction_diagnosis_correctness
field.description: Answer-key-derived verdict for the barrier diagnosis. Mechanical rule: `Correct` when `rec_succ_obstruction_identified = yes`, otherwise `Incorrect`.

field.name: overall_test02_correctness
field.description: Combined answer-key verdict. Mechanical rule: `Correct` when `completion_claim = no` and `rec_succ_obstruction_identified = yes`; `Unresolved` when `completion_claim = partial` or when `completion_claim = no` but the obstruction is not identified; `Incorrect` when `completion_claim = yes`.

field.name: quote_spawn_versus_answer_mismatch
field.description: QC flag column. Populated when a quote/spawn field does not match its corresponding coded answer field. Current populated value is `completion_claim` when `completion_claim_quote` conflicts with `completion_claim`; otherwise the field is left blank.

recordSet.name: Test 03 ordinal scaffold completion sessions
recordSet.description: 108 sessions completing a viable ordinal-valued measure scaffold for the remaining sorry cases in mu_decreases.

field.name: session_slug
field.description: Session identifier, `<model>__<timestamp>`.

field.name: model
field.description: Display model name.

field.name: provider
field.description: Model provider.

field.name: r_rec_succ_delivery
field.description: What the model literally delivered for the `R_rec_succ` branch. Current values in the finalized CSV are `closed_code`, `open_code`, `prose_only`, and `missing`.

field.name: r_eq_refl_delivery
field.description: What the model literally delivered for the `R_eq_refl` branch. Current values in the finalized CSV are `closed_code`, `open_code`, `prose_only`, and `missing`.

field.name: eq_refl_support_correctness
field.description: Derived answer-key verdict for the easy supportive branch. Mechanical rule: `Correct` when `r_eq_refl_delivery` is any non-`missing` value; otherwise `Incorrect`.

field.name: r_eq_diff_delivery
field.description: What the model literally delivered for the `R_eq_diff` branch. Current values in the finalized CSV are `closed_code`, `open_code`, `prose_only`, and `missing`.

field.name: hard_case_delivery_correctness
field.description: Derived answer-key verdict for the two theorem-backed hard obligations (`R_rec_succ`, `R_eq_diff`). Mechanical rule: `Correct` when both hard-case deliveries are `closed_code` or `open_code`; `Unresolved` when neither is `missing` but at least one is `prose_only`; `Incorrect` when either hard-case delivery is `missing`.

field.name: remaining_case_labels_correct
field.description: `yes` when the response correctly identifies the actual remaining target cases; `no` otherwise.

field.name: remaining_case_targeting_correctness
field.description: Derived answer-key verdict for the target-set identification. Mechanical rule: `Correct` when `remaining_case_labels_correct` is `yes`, otherwise `Incorrect`.

field.name: non_remaining_case_material_present
field.description: `yes` when the response spends substantive material on already-completed branches or unrelated case labels; `no` otherwise.

field.name: response_scope_correctness
field.description: Derived answer-key verdict for response focus. Mechanical rule: `Correct` when `non_remaining_case_material_present` is `no`, otherwise `Incorrect`.

field.name: overall_test03_correctness
field.description: Combined answer-key verdict for the response as a whole. Mechanical rule: `Correct` when hard-case delivery, easy-case support, targeting, and scope are all `Correct`; `Unresolved` when targeting and scope are `Correct`, the hard-case field is not `Incorrect`, but at least one required supportive condition still falls short; `Incorrect` otherwise.

recordSet.name: Test 04 measure verification sessions
recordSet.description: 108 sessions auditing a supplied (phase, cost) measure for KO7. Gold answer: unsound, with phase-exposure failure on R_merge_void_left.

field.name: session_slug
field.description: Session identifier, `<model>__<timestamp>`.

field.name: model
field.description: Display model name.

field.name: provider
field.description: Model provider.

field.name: measure_sound_yes_no
field.description: Final yes/no verdict on whether the proposed measure is sound.

field.name: measure_sound_quote
field.description: Verbatim quote supporting `measure_sound_yes_no`.

field.name: r_rec_succ_cited
field.description: `yes` when the response explicitly cites or analyzes `R_rec_succ`; `no` otherwise.

field.name: phase_exposure_cited
field.description: `yes` when the response explicitly identifies the wrapper-removal / exposure problem that can raise phase at the root; `no` otherwise.

field.name: self_correction_flag
field.description: `yes` when the response visibly revises or retracts an earlier claim; `no` otherwise.

field.name: self_contradiction_flag
field.description: `yes` when incompatible claims remain in the delivered response without a clean resolved reading; `no` otherwise.

field.name: review_notes
field.description: Free-text notes for follow-up, ambiguity, or short adjudication comments.

field.name: measure_sound_correctness
field.description: Derived verdict column. `Correct` when `measure_sound_yes_no = no`; `Incorrect` when `measure_sound_yes_no = yes`.

field.name: phase_exposure_localization_correctness
field.description: Derived verdict column. `Correct` when `phase_exposure_cited = yes`; `Incorrect` when `phase_exposure_cited = no`.

field.name: overall_test04_correctness
field.description: Derived verdict column. `Correct` when the response both rejects the measure and cites the wrapper-exposure failure, `Unresolved` when it rejects the measure without the decisive localization, and `Incorrect` when it says the measure is sound.

field.name: quote_spawn_versus_answer_mismatch
field.description: Helper QC column. Current live values are `yes` or `no`. It records whether a post-extraction quote/span review found the stored support text mismatched against the finalized answer field. It is not itself an answer-key verdict column.

recordSet.name: Test 05 candidate class reasoning sessions
recordSet.description: 108 sessions evaluating three additive measures mu1, mu2, mu3 for KO7. All three fail at R_rec_succ.

field.name: session_slug
field.description: Session identifier, `<model>__<timestamp>`.

field.name: model
field.description: Display model name.

field.name: provider
field.description: Model provider.

field.name: response_truncated_flag
field.description: `1` when the response is visibly truncated or materially incomplete; `0` otherwise.

field.name: tool_spill_flag
field.description: `1` when the response includes tool/process chatter rather than staying on the task; `0` otherwise.

field.name: mu1_yes_no
field.description: Final yes/no verdict for candidate `mu1`.

field.name: mu2_yes_no
field.description: Final yes/no verdict for candidate `mu2`.

field.name: mu3_yes_no
field.description: Final yes/no verdict for candidate `mu3`.

field.name: r_rec_succ_cited
field.description: `yes` when the response explicitly cites `R_rec_succ` or directly analyzes that rule; `no` otherwise.

field.name: self_correction_flag
field.description: `yes` when the response visibly revises or retracts an earlier claim; `no` otherwise.

field.name: self_contradiction_flag
field.description: `yes` when incompatible claims remain in the delivered response without a clean resolved reading; `no` otherwise.

field.name: adjudicator_notes
field.description: Free-text notes for ambiguity, follow-up, or short adjudication comments.

field.name: mu1_correctness
field.description: Derived verdict column. `Correct` when `mu1_yes_no = no`; `Incorrect` when `mu1_yes_no = yes`.

field.name: mu2_correctness
field.description: Derived verdict column. `Correct` when `mu2_yes_no = no`; `Incorrect` when `mu2_yes_no = yes`.

field.name: mu3_correctness
field.description: Derived verdict column. `Correct` when `mu3_yes_no = no`; `Incorrect` when `mu3_yes_no = yes`.

field.name: r_rec_succ_localization_correctness
field.description: Derived verdict column. `Correct` when `r_rec_succ_cited = yes`; `Incorrect` when `r_rec_succ_cited = no`.

field.name: overall_test05_correctness
field.description: Derived verdict column. `Correct` when all three candidate verdicts are `no` and the shared `R_rec_succ` obstruction is cited, `Unresolved` when all three candidate verdicts are `no` but the shared obstruction is not cited, and `Incorrect` otherwise.

recordSet.name: Test 06 branch realism sessions
recordSet.description: 108 sessions auditing a branch-realism helper strategy for KO7. Gold answer: unsound on the nested-delta branch.

field.name: session_slug
field.description: Session identifier, `<model>__<timestamp>`.

field.name: model
field.description: Display model name.

field.name: provider
field.description: Model provider.

field.name: condition
field.description: Prompt/run condition label. Current values in the finalized CSV are `run1`, `run2`, `run3`, `run4`, and `run5`.

field.name: strategy_sound_verdict
field.description: Overall verdict on whether the helper strategy is sound as written.

field.name: strategy_sound_correctness
field.description: Derived answer-key verdict for the overall strategy claim. Mechanical rule: `Correct` when `strategy_sound_verdict` is `unsound`, otherwise `Incorrect`.

field.name: strategy_sound_quote
field.description: Verbatim quote supporting `strategy_sound_verdict`.

field.name: kappa_rec_delta_step_verdict
field.description: What the response says about `kappa_rec_delta_step`.

field.name: kappa_rec_delta_step_correctness
field.description: Derived answer-key verdict for `kappa_rec_delta_step`. Mechanical rule: `Correct` when `kappa_rec_delta_step_verdict` is `fails`, otherwise `Incorrect`.

field.name: kappa_rec_succ_drop_verdict
field.description: What the response says about `kappa_rec_succ_drop`.

field.name: kappa_rec_succ_drop_correctness
field.description: Derived answer-key verdict for `kappa_rec_succ_drop`. Mechanical rule: `Correct` when `kappa_rec_succ_drop_verdict` is `fails`, otherwise `Incorrect`.

field.name: n_equals_delta_m_cited
field.description: `yes` when the response explicitly raises the critical branch case where `n` itself can be another `delta` term; `no` otherwise.

field.name: nested_delta_branch_diagnosis_correctness
field.description: Derived answer-key verdict for the critical-branch diagnosis. Mechanical rule: `Correct` when `n_equals_delta_m_cited` is `yes`, otherwise `Incorrect`.

field.name: first_named_failure_point
field.description: The first explicit failure point named by the response.

field.name: failure_localization_quality
field.description: Derived answer-key quality label for `first_named_failure_point`. Mechanical rule: `Correct` when the first named failure point is `kappa_rec_delta_step`, `Unresolved` when it is `kappa_rec_succ_drop`, and `Incorrect` otherwise.

field.name: concrete_counterexample_provided
field.description: `yes` when the response provides a concrete instantiation or worked counterexample; `no` otherwise.

field.name: counterexample_support_correctness
field.description: Derived answer-key verdict for counterexample support. Mechanical rule: `Correct` when `concrete_counterexample_provided` is `yes`, otherwise `Incorrect`.

field.name: overall_test06_correctness
field.description: Combined answer-key verdict for Test 06. Mechanical rule: `Correct` when the strategy is judged `unsound`, both helper verdicts are `fails`, and the nested-`delta` branch is cited; `Unresolved` when the overall strategy and both helper-failure verdicts are correct but the nested-`delta` branch is not cited; `Incorrect` otherwise.

field.name: quote_spawn_versus_answer_mismatch
field.description: Helper QC column. Current live values are `yes` or `no`. It records whether a post-extraction quote/span review found the stored support text mismatched against the finalized answer field. It is not itself an answer-key verdict column.
